name: Value of the fine-structure constant
summary: The fine-structure constant alpha = e^2 / (4 pi epsilon_0 hbar c) ~ 1/137.036 has a specific numerical value that is, in the SM + GR framework, simply a free parameter without explanation. Eddington's 1929 attempt to derive 1/137 from pure number theory was a notable failure; modern attempts (string-landscape, anthropic) replace derivation with selection. The puzzle is whether alpha is a derivable constant or an environmental input.

characteristic_numbers.note: Equivalent to alpha^-1 = 137.035999084. Measured to ~ 10^-10 precision via Cs-fountain and Rb-fountain atom interferometry.
Running of alpha brings it close to alpha_2 and alpha_3 near the GUT scale; the unified alpha_GUT ~ 1/24 has no first-principles derivation either.

principles_stressed.note: alpha is technically natural (small relative to 1, no symmetry argument needed), but the specific value has no derivation. Anthropic arguments (Davies, Barrow-Tipler) suggest a window |alpha - 1/137| / alpha < few % is required for complex chemistry; this is an upper bound on tuning, not a derivation. String landscape replaces 'why this value' with 'this is the value the multiverse selects in our patch'.

history: Eddington (1929) attempted derivation from group theory and degrees of freedom; widely viewed as numerology after his failed 1/136 prediction. Modern variation bounds (Webb et al. 2001, 2011 on quasar absorbers; atomic-clock comparisons) constrain delta alpha / alpha < 10^-7 over the age of the universe.

references.citation: Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics' - Physical Constants section, Prog. Theor. Exp. Phys. 2024, 083C01 (2024)
